fr.recommendations: Improving emergency responder access and movement can be achieved by optimizing the current layout and clearing obstructions. Adding an additional access point could facilitate quicker entry and exit, reducing response times. Enhancing or creating access pathways at the rear of the property may support more comprehensive coverage during operations, particularly when moving large equipment. Managing the dense vegetation around all travel pathways and near the perimeter of the property may enhance visibility, allowing responders to navigate more effectively and safely. Furthermore, maintaining a clear and open area in the landscaped or green spaces would support staging activities and equipment placement, ensuring that responders have adequate room to maneuver as necessary. Clearly marking access points and pathways could assist in rapid navigation and coordination during emergencies.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the structure by increasing the separation between vegetation and buildings may reduce exposure potential. Thinning dense tree canopies and managing vegetation directly adjacent to the structure could help decrease fuel continuity and lower fire intensity near the structure. Creating breaks in the continuous grass cover and reducing dense shrub cover at strategic locations could decrease the risk of fire spread across the landscape. Maintaining the vegetated perimeter with proper spacing and selective thinning may improve not only defensible space but also provide clear access for firefighters during suppression activities. Strategically managing vegetation within and around the vegetated drainage can enhance moisture retention and create a natural firebreak, coinciding with the benefits of existing open green spaces. By prioritizing these actions, the property may improve its preparedness and resilience against wildfire events.
